_meta.topic: Identify Data Sources Needed for a Solution
_meta.description: Data source discovery, connector availability, data residency requirements, and volume/velocity assessment.

mcq.q: Why should a solution architect identify data sources early when evaluating a proposed Power Platform or Dynamics 365 solution?
options: To decide which users should get dark mode
To understand what data is needed, where it lives, who owns it, how it moves, and what constraints shape the solution
To avoid documenting integrations
To guarantee that all data will move into Dataverse
mcq.explanation: Early data source discovery helps the architect understand source systems, ownership, quality, movement, and constraints such as integration, residency, and performance expectations.
wrongExplanations: UI preference is unrelated to the main reason for data source discovery.
Data source discovery usually increases the need to document integrations, not avoid them.
Not all data belongs in Dataverse; some data may remain in other systems or be accessed through integration.

msRef.label: Study guide for Exam PL-600: Microsoft Power Platform Solution Architect

mcq.q: Which discovery question best helps identify whether a data source is critical to the proposed solution?
options: What color is the current application's header?
What business process depends on this data, who owns it, and what happens if it is delayed or unavailable?
How many users like the current report layout?
What font is used in the source system?
mcq.explanation: The strongest discovery question ties the data source to business process dependency, ownership, and operational impact, which reveals whether it is truly critical.
wrongExplanations: Visual design does not establish business criticality.
Report layout preferences do not reveal operational dependency.
Font choice is irrelevant.

msRef.label: Discover customer needs as a Solution Architect for Dynamics 365 and Microsoft Power Platform

mcq.q: A stakeholder says, "We need data from the ERP in the new solution." What should the architect clarify next?
options: Only the ERP vendor name
Which entities are needed, who owns them, how current they must be, and whether they need to be copied or accessed through integration
Whether the ERP has a blue theme
Only how many users log in daily
mcq.explanation: The architect needs to understand data entities, ownership, freshness, and access pattern. Those factors drive integration strategy, synchronization design, and solution scope.
wrongExplanations: Vendor name alone does not define the data requirement.
Theme is irrelevant to data architecture.
Daily login count does not define the data access pattern.

msRef.label: Integrate your Dynamics 365 apps with other solutions

mcq.q: What is the strongest reason to assess connector availability during data source discovery?
options: To choose the most visually attractive connector
To determine whether the needed systems can be connected through supported patterns or whether alternative approaches are required
To avoid governance review
To ensure every integration becomes real time
mcq.explanation: Connector availability affects feasibility, effort, governance, and pattern choice. If no suitable supported connector exists, the architect may need a different integration approach.
wrongExplanations: Visual appearance has nothing to do with architectural suitability.
Connector use still needs governance review.
Not every integration should be real time.

msRef.label: Connectors overview

mcq.q: Why is connector availability alone not enough when assessing data sources?
options: Because the connector icon may change later
Because security, governance, data residency, limits, licensing, and operational fit also matter
Because connectors never work in production
Because custom connectors are always forbidden
mcq.explanation: A connector existing is only part of the picture. The architect also needs to assess governance constraints, data movement rules, licensing, security posture, and operational suitability.
wrongExplanations: Icon changes are irrelevant to architectural evaluation.
Connectors can work in production when used appropriately.
Custom connectors are not always forbidden, but they do require governance and design review.

msRef.label: Data policies - Power Platform

mcq.q: A customer has a requirement that customer data must remain in a specific geography. What should the architect assess first?
options: Whether users prefer faster dashboards
Whether the environment region, data residency model, and connected services align with that geography requirement
Whether the case form has too many tabs
Whether the CRM sitemap is modern
mcq.explanation: A geography requirement means the architect must validate region choice, data residency behavior, and whether all relevant services align with that requirement.
wrongExplanations: Dashboard preference does not address residency compliance.
Form tab count is irrelevant.
UI structure does not resolve geography constraints.

msRef.label: Where your Microsoft Dynamics 365 and Power Platform customer data is stored

mcq.q: Which statement best reflects good architect judgment about data residency in Power Platform?
options: Residency matters only after go-live
Residency should be evaluated early because region choice and connected services can affect compliance and design
Residency is only a concern for infrastructure teams
Residency can be ignored if a connector exists
mcq.explanation: Data residency affects design choices from the beginning. Region selection and service use can create compliance implications that must be understood early.
wrongExplanations: That is too late; residency can influence core design and approval decisions.
It is broader than infrastructure and affects application architecture too.
Connector availability does not override residency obligations.

msRef.label: Choose the region when setting up an environment

mcq.q: Why should the architect assess data volume and velocity during data source discovery?
options: To choose a better font for reports
To understand whether the proposed data handling pattern is realistic for throughput, synchronization, and performance expectations
To eliminate the need for data governance
To guarantee that all data must be stored in the same place
mcq.explanation: Data volume and velocity can materially affect migration strategy, synchronization design, batching, performance, and architecture feasibility.
wrongExplanations: Formatting is irrelevant to volume and velocity assessment.
Governance still matters regardless of throughput needs.
Different data may belong in different systems or movement patterns.

msRef.label: Overview for implementation workshops to provide guidance

mcq.q: A requirement calls for high-volume near-real-time updates from an on-premises source into Dynamics 365. Which concern should the architect raise first?
options: Whether the welcome screen is branded
Whether latency, batching strategy, connectivity pattern, and realistic throughput have been assessed
Whether users prefer dark mode
Whether the source server uses a recent browser
mcq.explanation: High-volume near-real-time integration from on-premises sources raises latency and throughput concerns that should be assessed early, along with realistic batching and connectivity patterns.
wrongExplanations: Branding is irrelevant.
User theme preference does not affect integration feasibility.
Browser age is not the key architectural issue in this scenario.

msRef.label: Overcome integration challenges in Dynamics 365 projects

mcq.q: Which statement best shows that the architect is evaluating data sources at the right level?
options: We listed every source system by name
We identified what data is needed, who owns it, how often it changes, what constraints apply, and how it supports business processes
We counted the number of tables in each source
We copied the source system screenshots into the design pack
mcq.explanation: Good data source evaluation ties technical source information to ownership, usage, change frequency, constraints, and business relevance.
wrongExplanations: Names alone are not enough.
Object counts do not provide enough business or architectural meaning.
Screenshots do not substitute for architectural analysis.

msRef.label: Study guide for Exam PL-600: Microsoft Power Platform Solution Architect

tf.q: True or False: A supported connector automatically means the data source is acceptable from a governance and compliance perspective.
tf.explanation: A connector may exist, but governance, DLP policies, licensing, residency, and security requirements still need to be evaluated.

msRef.label: Data policies - Power Platform

tf.q: True or False: Data residency requirements can affect environment region selection and service design choices.
tf.explanation: Region and geography choices influence where customer data is stored and can affect whether the solution aligns with residency requirements.

msRef.label: Where your Microsoft Dynamics 365 and Power Platform customer data is stored

tf.q: True or False: Data volume and throughput considerations are only relevant during production tuning, not during solution assessment.
tf.explanation: Volume and throughput should be assessed early because they influence migration approach, integration pattern, performance expectations, and feasibility.

msRef.label: Overview for implementation workshops to provide guidance

tf.q: True or False: A data source that is not stored in Dataverse can still be essential to the final solution.
tf.explanation: Many solutions rely on external systems as systems of record or integrated data sources. Not all relevant data needs to be copied into Dataverse.

msRef.label: Integrate your Dynamics 365 apps with other solutions

dragdrop.instruction: Match each architect concern to the most relevant data source assessment area.
items: Is there a supported way to connect to the required service?
Must customer data remain in a particular geography?
Will millions of updates arrive daily from the source?
Who is the authoritative owner of customer credit status?
dragdrop.explanation: Data source discovery should separate concerns around connectivity, geography, throughput, and ownership because each affects solution direction differently.

msRef.label: Connectors overview

dragdrop.instruction: Match each discovery action to the reason it matters.
items: Estimate data volume and change frequency
zones: Clarifies who governs quality, access, and accountability
Shows whether the source can be used through approved patterns
Helps determine whether geography and compliance needs can be met
Helps validate whether the proposed synchronization pattern is feasible

correctMapping.Identify data owners: Clarifies who governs quality, access, and accountability
correctMapping.Review connector and policy constraints: Shows whether the source can be used through approved patterns
correctMapping.Assess region and residency expectations: Helps determine whether geography and compliance needs can be met
correctMapping.Estimate data volume and change frequenc: Helps validate whether the proposed synchronization pattern is feasible

dragdrop.explanation: The architect needs to connect each discovery step to a concrete architectural purpose instead of treating data source inventory as a checklist exercise.

msRef.label: How to use the Power Platform Well-Architected Framework

casestudy.title: Case Study - Choosing Data Sources Without Getting Burned Later
casestudy.scenario: A company wants a new service solution in Dynamics 365. During discovery, stakeholders say the solution needs customer data from the ERP, warranty history from a legacy database, shipment status from a logistics platform, and field inspection photos from a separate document service. The business expects shipment status updates to appear quickly, and field teams in one region say customer data must remain in a specific geography. The delivery team finds a prebuilt connector for the logistics platform, but the organization's data policies are strict. The source systems also vary heavily in record volume and update frequency.

msRef.label: Study guide for Exam PL-600: Microsoft Power Platform Solution Architect

questions.q: What should the architect do first?
options: Assume all source data should be copied into Dataverse
Identify the required data sets, owners, freshness needs, and constraints for each source before choosing patterns
Ignore the legacy database because it is not modern
Start building flows from the logistics connector immediately
questions.explanation: The first step is to understand what data is needed, who owns it, how current it must be, and what constraints apply. Pattern choices should follow that analysis.
wrongExplanations: Copying everything into Dataverse by default is poor architect judgment.
Legacy systems may still contain critical business data.
Immediate implementation is premature before constraints are understood.

msRef.label: Discover customer needs as a Solution Architect for Dynamics 365 and Microsoft Power Platform

questions.q: Why is the existence of a prebuilt connector for the logistics platform not enough on its own?
options: Because connector icons may change
Because governance policies, data movement rules, and operational fit still need evaluation
Because prebuilt connectors cannot be used in enterprise projects
Because shipment data never needs frequent updates
questions.explanation: A connector being available does not mean the pattern is automatically acceptable. Governance, policy, and operational requirements still matter.
wrongExplanations: Icon changes are irrelevant.
Prebuilt connectors can be used in enterprise projects when appropriate.
Some shipment scenarios absolutely do need timely updates.

msRef.label: Data policies - Power Platform

questions.q: What is the main architectural implication of the requirement that customer data remain in a specific geography?
options: Only the dashboard layout is affected
Environment region, service choices, and data movement design may need to align to residency expectations
The solution must avoid all connectors
The app cannot use Dynamics 365
questions.explanation: Geography requirements influence region selection, service design, and how data moves across systems and environments.
wrongExplanations: Layout is unrelated.
Residency requirements do not automatically forbid all connectors.
Dynamics 365 can still be used; the design just needs to align with the requirement.

msRef.label: Choose the region when setting up an environment

questions.q: Why should the architect assess record volume and update frequency for these sources before recommending a design?
options: Because it affects whether throughput, batching, latency, and synchronization expectations are realistic
Because it decides the app's icon color
Because it removes the need for requirements refinement
Because it proves the ERP should be replaced
questions.explanation: Data volume and frequency directly influence whether a proposed pattern can meet performance and operational expectations realistically.
wrongExplanations: Icon color is irrelevant.
Requirements refinement is still needed.
Volume alone does not prove the ERP should be replaced.

msRef.label: Overview for implementation workshops to provide guidance
